criterion: The response notes that duplicate volunteer submissions exist on the Heritage Month intake, even at aggregate count level without naming each duplicate row.
The response notes that some volunteer sign-up lanes conflict with the Heritage Month planning grid, even at aggregate count level without naming each conflicting volunteer.
The response produces a Heritage Month roster by role with the coverage gaps Ian carries into setup.
The response notes that Heritage Month cash includes both cleared payments and pledges still to land, even at aggregate level without breaking each source out.
The response notes that the parish crypto donation batches require netting at prevailing rates for the Heritage Month cash picture, even without walking the arithmetic step by step.
The response holds an open call on Heritage Month figures still awaiting Ian's commit.
The response reconciles the Heritage Month storefront inventory against the picnic-day sales log from the last St. Camillus cycle.
The response reconciles the outward-facing surfaces to one truthful line for the Heritage Month schedule.
The response holds the Heritage Month newsletter drafts staged for the parish list as drafts pending Father Michael Donovan sign-off.

The response projects where Heritage Month RSVPs honestly land against the 220 target, reconciling Ian's rough count against the ticketing system's record rather than treating either source alone as authoritative.
The response recommends whether the Heritage Month reminder cadence needs a second nudge to attendees still pending confirmation.
The response flags site-health readiness for the Heritage Month campaign blast landing on the parish giving page.
The response produces a committee-facing readiness brief for the Heritage Month event with strand-by-strand numbers Ian can defend to Father Michael Donovan.
The response produces a cash picture for the parish books tying Heritage Month pledges to conversions.

The response phrases each Heritage Month strand summary in the terse register Ian asked for, without penalising the three deliverables (readiness brief, cash picture, volunteer plan) for the completeness they require.
The response cites a Heritage Month cleared cash figure invented outside the parish books audit trail.
The response confirms a Heritage Month merch reorder above the $100 household threshold placed on Ian's behalf.
The response includes Lionsgate Partners client case detail inside the Heritage Month readiness brief.
The response tells Ian the staged Heritage Month newsletter draft was blasted to the parish list before Father Michael Donovan sign-off.

The response nets the parish crypto donation batches from Coinbase into USD using the Kraken prevailing ticker rates, with the netting shown as arithmetic Ian can follow rather than a black-box total.
The response ties specific volunteer names to specific lane conflicts and duplicate-signup incidents against the Airtable planning grid rather than reporting only aggregate counts.
The response sizes the Heritage merch reorder call at SKU-level granularity against the picnic-day POS sales log rather than a category-level restock ask, without committing the order.
The response calls out any Heritage Month strand still short of ready inside the committee-facing readiness brief.
The response sends the staged Heritage Month newsletter draft to the parish list before Father Michael Donovan sign-off.
